attractions.name: Capitol Lake
attractions.description: Capitol Lake is a 260-acre artificial lake located at the base of the Washington State Capitol Campus in Olympia. Created in 1951 as a freshwater reflecting pool for the state's legislative building, it serves as a popular urban recreational area featuring scenic walking trails and diverse wildlife. The lake is managed by the Washington State Department of Enterprise Services and is the focus of long-term environmental restoration efforts.
attractions.address: 330 5th Ave SW, Olympia, WA 98501 (Heritage Park entrance)
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (Winter: Nov 1 – Mar 31); open until 9:00 PM or dusk in summer months.
highlights: ['Scenic 1.7-mile paved loop trail popular for walking, running, and cycling.', 'Iconic reflections of the Washington State Capitol dome on the calm lake surface.', 'Wildlife viewing opportunities, including year-round waterfowl and seasonal salmon runs.', 'Direct access to Heritage Park and Marathon Park waterfront areas.', "Interpretive markers detailing the area's history and the Deschutes Estuary.", "Proximity to downtown Olympia's shops, cafes, and historical landmarks."]
attractions.tips: The best time for photography is during the 'golden hour' at sunrise or sunset to capture the Capitol's reflection. Note that swimming and boating are currently prohibited to protect the ecosystem. Free parking is available at Marathon Park and nearby street locations, though spots can fill quickly during weekday business hours.
attractions.nearby: Washington State Capitol Building, Brewery Park at Tumwater Falls, Olympia Farmers Market, Percival Landing Park

attractions.name: Heritage Park & Capitol Lake Trails
attractions.description: Heritage Park is a 24-acre state-owned park in Olympia that serves as a beautiful link between the Capitol Campus and downtown. It features a lakeside loop trail, expansive lawns, and panoramic views of the water and the historic Legislative Building. (Washington State Capitol Campus)
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 7:00 AM - Dusk (Washington State Capitol Campus)

attractions.tips: Free parking is available in a small lot off 5th Avenue SW (15 stalls), and metered street parking is free after 5 p.m. and on weekends. The trail is wheelchair accessible and highly popular for jogging and birdwatching. (Washington State Capitol Campus, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Washington State Capitol Building, Marathon Park, Percival Landing Park, Hands On Children's Museum, Sylvester Park, and downtown Olympia shops and restaurants (Washington State Capitol Campus, Waze)

attractions.name: Korean War Memorial (also known as Washington State Korean War Memorial)
attractions.description: The Korean War Memorial is a somber tribute featuring a bronze sculpture of three battle-weary soldiers huddled around a campfire on muddied terrain. Dedicated in 1993, it was the first state-sponsored Korean War memorial in the United States and honors the 122,000 Washingtonians who served and those who lost their lives in the "Forgotten War." (Washington State Capitol Campus, Experience Olympia)
attractions.address: 416 Sid Snyder Ave SW (East Capitol Campus Plaza, near 14th Ave SE & Capitol Way S)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Note: Capitol Campus buildings have limited hours, but outdoor memorials are typically accessible at all times) (National Park Service - general memorial policy, Washington State Capitol Campus)
attractions.admission: Free (Washington State Capitol Campus, Experience Olympia)
highlights: 'Features a bronze sculpture of three soldiers in rain gear huddling around a campfire, created by artist Deborah Copenhaver Fellows ([Washington State Capitol Campus)', 'Backdropped by 22 flags representing the nations of the UN coalition that participated in the war (ThurstonTalk)', 'Includes stone tablets inscribed with the names of over 530 Washington residents killed in action (Washington State Capitol Campus)', 'The first state-sponsored Korean War memorial in the U.S. (Washington State Capitol Campus)', 'Unique location on the East Capitol Campus, situated atop a parking garage (Washington State Capitol Campus)', 'The sculpture was notably airlifted into place by a helicopter because the rooftop could not support a crane (Washington State Capitol Campus)']
attractions.tips: The memorial is located on the East Campus, which is connected to the main West Campus by a pedestrian skybridge over Capitol Way; it is often overlooked by visitors who stay on the main campus. Visit during or after rain to see the 'muddy terrain' base of the sculpture create puddles as intended by the artist. (ThurstonTalk, Washington State Capitol Campus)

It functions as a modern 'corner store' offering a curated selection of artisanal condiments, local farm products, and unique prepared foods like 'salad bribes' and Korean-inspired Bo-ssam sandwiches. attractions.name: Olympia Symphony Orchestra
attractions.description: The Olympia Symphony Orchestra is a professional ensemble based in Olympia, Washington, that has served the South Puget Sound region since its founding in 1947. Its mission is to provide high-quality live music to enrich, educate, and entertain an expanding audience through a diverse range of seasonal performances (Olympia Symphony).

attractions.category: Historic District / Residential Neighborhood South Capitol Neighborhood Association
attractions.description: The South Capitol Neighborhood Historic District is one of Olympia's oldest residential areas, featuring over 440 primary properties listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1991. The neighborhood is renowned for its diverse early-20th century architecture, including Craftsman and Victorian styles, and serves as a popular residential area for government workers due to its proximity to the State Capitol. It is characterized by its walkable, tree-lined streets and strong community spirit. South Capitol Neighborhood Association, Homes.com

attractions.name: State Capital Museum (Washington State Capital Museum and Outreach Center)
attractions.description: The State Capital Museum is housed in the historic Lord Mansion, a 1923 Spanish Colonial Revival villa designed by Joseph Wohleb. It served as a primary repository for Washington’s political and social history, featuring exhibits on Native American culture, the legislative process, and the development of Olympia as the state capital. Although the museum itself closed to the public in 2014, the building remains a significant historical landmark in the South Capitol neighborhood. Olympia Historical Society, Wonderful Museums
attractions.hours: Permanently Closed. Historical Hours: Wednesday–Saturday, 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM. Seattle's Child, Evergreen State College
attractions.admission: Historical: Adults $2, Students $1, Families $5, Children 6 and under Free. Current status: Permanently Closed. Seattle's Child, Tripadvisor
highlights: ['Historic Lord Mansion architecture (1923 Georgian/Spanish Colonial Revival style)', "Governors' Gallery featuring portraits and biographies of past Washington leaders", "Exhibits on the Women's Suffrage movement and social change in Washington", 'Replica of a Salish Tribal Winter House and Native American heritage displays', 'Statehood and early territorial development artifacts', 'Intricate interior woodwork and a grand central staircase preserved from the original residence']
attractions.tips: The museum is no longer open for regular tours as it closed in 2014; however, the exterior architecture and gardens can still be viewed from the street. Visitors interested in current Washington state history exhibits should visit the Washington State History Museum in Tacoma. ThurstonTalk, Seattle's Child

attractions.description: The Tivoli Fountain is a magnificent water feature on the Washington State Capitol campus and a replica of a Roman-style fountain in Copenhagen's Tivoli Gardens. It features an outer ring of 540 jets that create a water umbrella, surrounding two inner rings of vertical sprays and a central spout that shoots water 25 feet into the air (Washington State Capitol Campus).

attractions.name: Washington State Capitol (Legislative Building)
attractions.description: The Washington State Capitol, also known as the Legislative Building, is the seat of government for the state of Washington and home to the State Legislature. Completed in 1928, the building features the tallest self-supporting masonry dome in North America, reaching a height of 287 feet. It is the center of a larger administrative campus that includes the Governor's Mansion, the Temple of Justice, and several state office buildings.

Rainier']
attractions.tips: Free 50-minute guided tours are available daily; reservations are recommended but not required for individuals. The grounds are open from dawn to dusk. Visitor parking is $2 per hour on weekdays (7 a.m. – 5 p.m.) and free on evenings, weekends, and state holidays.

attractions.name: Washington State Governor's Mansion
attractions.description: Built in 1908 and first occupied in 1909, the Washington State Governor's Mansion is the oldest building on the state Capitol's West Campus. This 19-room Georgian-style residence has been fully restored and is furnished with a significant collection of antiques from the American Federal period. It serves as the official residence for Washington's governors and is maintained by the non-profit Governor's Mansion Foundation. Governor's Mansion Foundation, HistoryLink

Designed by the Portico Group, the memorial features granite walls inscribed with the names of fallen officers, providing a place for survivors to remember their loved ones and for the public to reflect on the sacrifices made to protect them. It is situated in a beautiful setting on the Capitol Campus, overlooking Capitol Lake and downtown Olympia Behind the Badge Foundation, Experience Olympia.

Completed in 1920 and recently renovated in 2024, the solemn two-story classical revival structure is the oldest public building on the campus and host to approximately 150 judicial rulings annually. attractions.name: World War II Memorial
attractions.description: The World War II Memorial in Olympia is a poignant tribute honoring the nearly 6,000 Washington residents who lost their lives during the war. Designed by Simon Kogan, the memorial features five 14-foot-high bronze blades that form a star-like cluster mirroring the shape of the nearby Capitol dome. Each blade is etched with the names of the fallen, which remarkably form ghostly silhouette images of military personnel and civilians when viewed from a distance (Experience Olympia, Clio).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Outdoor public memorial) (Experience Olympia)

attractions.tips: Visit during daylight hours to clearly see the ghostly figures formed by the names on the bronze blades. Free parking is available at the Olympia Farmers Market with a free DASH shuttle to the Capitol Campus, or use on-campus metered parking for $2 per hour (Experience Olympia).

attractions.address: City Hall, 601 4th Ave E, Olympia, WA 98501 (Map start), or various locations in Downtown Olympia
attractions.hours: Daily: 24/7 (Outdoor public art) (Experience Olympia)
attractions.phone: 360.709.2678 (City of Olympia Arts Commission) (City of Olympia)
highlights: 'Olympia-Rafah Solidarity Mural: A 4,000 sq. ft. interdisciplinary mural celebrating those who struggle for justice ([Experience Olympia).', "Respect & Love Olympia: An iconic rainbow-themed mural representing the community's inclusive spirit (Experience Olympia).", "The Great Wave off Kanagawa: A beautiful reproduction of Hokusai's famous work, originally painted in 1977 (City of Olympia).", 'Holding Up Olympia: A collaborative piece where participants painted their own representations, including local dogs (ThurstonTalk).', 'Super Hero Wall: A fun mural featuring a wide array of popular comic book heroes (Experience Olympia).']
attractions.tips: The tour is approximately a half-mile long and follows mostly flat sidewalks; however, some murals are located in alleys with unlevel terrain (ThurstonTalk). Use the official interactive StoryMap on a smartphone to easily navigate and learn the stories behind each piece (City of Olympia).

attractions.name: Olympia Artesian Well (Experience Olympia)
attractions.category: Natural Wonder / Public Park (Experience Olympia, Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Olympia Artesian Well is a historic landmark providing free-flowing, cold, and refreshing water from a natural aquifer. Adorned with a vibrant blue and white tile mosaic, it serves as a unique community gathering spot and a symbol of the city's heritage, famously associated with the 'It's the Water' slogan of the former Olympia Brewing Company (Experience Olympia, ThurstonTalk).
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (well and restroom access) (The Olympian, Atlas Obscura)
attractions.phone: (360) 753-8380 (City of Olympia Parks, Arts & Recreation) (City of Olympia)
highlights: 'Free access to natural, cold artesian water from a deep aquifer ([Atlas Obscura)', 'Iconic cobalt and white mosaic art installation around the spout (Experience Olympia)', 'Historically significant site used by steam locomotives in the early 1900s (ThurstonTalk)', "Designated 'filling station' parking spots for visitors with large containers (Atlas Obscura)", 'Central downtown location that serves as a unique cultural meeting point (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Bring your own jugs or bottles as the water is free and plentiful. Be aware that the water has naturally high manganese levels; boiling it is not recommended as it concentrates the mineral. While the well remains open 24/7, the surrounding Artesian Commons park area has been fenced off since 2018 (Atlas Obscura, The Olympian, Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Hands On Children's Museum, Percival Landing Park, Olympia Farmers Market, Well 80 Brewhouse (Tripadvisor, ThurstonTalk)

attractions.name: Olympia Downtown Historic District
attractions.description: The Olympia Downtown Historic District is a vibrant 17-block area listed on the National Register of Historic Places, featuring 51 contributing properties built primarily between 1911 and 1930. This bustling urban hub blends historic, mid-century, and contemporary architecture with a diverse array of eclectic shops, local dining, and cultural landmarks. It serves as the heart of Washington's capital, offering visitors a unique mix of historical significance and modern Pacific Northwest charm.
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; Olympia Downtown Alliance Office: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed. Individual business hours vary.
highlights: ['Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 2004, covering 17 historic blocks.', 'Features 51 contributing properties, including the iconic Old Capitol Building and Sylvester Park.', 'Showcases diverse architectural styles such as Romanesque and Beaux-Arts, many designed by Joseph Wohleb.', 'Home to a vibrant arts scene with numerous public murals and the biannual Arts Walk event.', 'Direct access to the scenic Budd Inlet waterfront via Percival Landing.', 'Boasts a dense collection of independent shops, local breweries, and acclaimed farm-to-table restaurants.']
attractions.tips: Take a self-guided mural tour starting at the Traditions Fair Trade Gallery to see the city's extensive street art collection. Visit during the spring or fall Arts Walk to experience the district at its most vibrant. Start your historical exploration at Sylvester Park, which serves as a central landmark for many of the district's most significant buildings. Use the 'LoveOly' program to discover local business promotions and events.

It is a bustling marketplace featuring over 120 vendors from four local counties, offering fresh produce, artisan crafts, and eight on-site restaurants in a vibrant waterfront setting (Official Website, Explore Washington State).

attractions.name: The Washington Center for the Performing Arts (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Washington Center for the Performing Arts is the South Puget Sound's largest performing arts facility, committed to providing a wide variety of entertainment and cultural activities. Originally opened as the Liberty Theater in 1924 and renovated in 1985, it serves as a community gathering place in the heart of Washington's capital city, hosting national and international touring artists across various genres (The Washington Center).
attractions.address: 512 Washington St SE, Olympia, WA 98501, USA (The Washington Center)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Tuesday-Friday 12:00 PM-5:30 PM, Saturday 12:00 PM-4:00 PM. Also open two hours before every ticketed event (The Washington Center).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Saturday (Box Office hours), plus performance days (The Washington Center)
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event; for example, Ballet Northwest's Swan Lake ranges from $24 to $47 across youth, student, senior, and adult tiers (Event Details). Discounts are available for military, seniors, and students on select shows (Box Office Information).
highlights: 'Hosts a diverse season of national and international touring artists including dance, music, comedy, and theater ([The Washington Center).', 'Home to the historic Andy Crow Wurlitzer Organ, maintained for regular performances (The Washington Center).', 'Features a Main Stage with nearly 1,000 seats and a more intimate Black Box Theater (Experience Olympia).', 'The facility is a renovated 1924 theater (formerly the Liberty and Olympic theaters) that serves as a downtown cultural anchor (Olympia Historical Society).', 'Offers creative connections through educational outreach and an art gallery featuring regional artists (The Washington Center).']
attractions.tips: Street parking in downtown Olympia is free on weekends and after 5:00 PM on weekdays, but can be limited during major events (City of Olympia Parking). Arrive early as doors typically open between 90 minutes and two hours before showtime (Vivid Seats). Look for the historic WWI-era street clock outside the entrance (Olympia Historical Society).
attractions.nearby: Heritage Park, Percival Landing Park, the Washington State Capitol Building, and numerous downtown restaurants and boutiques (Experience Olympia)

attractions.name: Three Magnets Brewing Co.
attractions.description: Three Magnets Brewing Co. is a prominent microbrewery and community hub in Downtown Olympia, established in 2014. It is known for its wide selection of craft beers, including its pioneering "Self Care" line of non-alcoholic brews, and features an all-ages dining area with a rotation of local food trucks. The space is designed to be family and pet-friendly, offering both indoor and outdoor seating along with games like cornhole.
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Fri-Sat: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Sun: 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM Official Website
highlights: ['Pioneer of the "Self Care" line of premium non-alcoholic craft beers.', 'Family and dog-friendly environment with an all-ages dining area and a 21+ bar.', "Hosts a variety of local food trucks on-site, such as O.G.'s Smokehouse BBQ.", 'Features reservable cornhole lanes and other interactive games.', 'Collaborates with local businesses for unique brews, like the Rainy Day IPA.', 'Offers a seasonal outdoor beer garden and cozy indoor seating.']
attractions.tips: Check the social media pages for O.G.'s Smokehouse BBQ for their specific daily hours, as they may differ from the pub's. While cornhole is free to play, it is recommended to reserve a lane online during peak hours. The brewery is highly family-friendly, making it a great stop for those traveling with children. ThurstonTalk, Experience Olympia
attractions.nearby: Washington State Capitol, Percival Landing Park, Hands On Children's Museum, Olympia Farmers Market Experience Olympia

attractions.name: Watershed Park
attractions.category: Public Park, Temperate Rain Forest (City of Olympia, Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Watershed Park is a 153-acre temperate rain forest located in the heart of Olympia. It features the Moxlie Creek Springs Basin, one of the largest spring basins in the region, and offers a network of trails through lush wetlands and towering canopies. Historically, the area served as the primary water source for the city before being preserved as a public park following a landmark citizen-led legal battle. (City of Olympia, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: 8:00 a.m. - 9:00 p.m. daily (Experience Olympia)
highlights: "Moxlie Creek Springs Basin, one of the region's largest spring basins ([City of Olympia)", 'G. Eldon Marshall Trail, a 1.5-mile loop through the forest (Experience Olympia)', 'Visible remnants of the historic 19th-century waterworks and pumping facilities (Wikipedia)', 'Lush temperate rain forest canopy with skunk cabbage and salmonberries (City of Olympia)', 'Extensive network of boardwalks and bridges over wetlands (Washington Trails Association)']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy shoes as the trails can be steep, stepped, and sometimes damp due to the springs. Parking is available at the main Henderson Blvd entrance, but additional pedestrian entrances are located on Eastside Street and 22nd Avenue. The trail from 22nd Avenue is considered the most accessible route to the creek viewing platform. (City of Olympia)
attractions.nearby: Olympia Coffee Roasting Co., Wildwood Neighborhood shops, Hands On Children's Museum, Washington State Capitol Campus (Experience Olympia, Homes.com)

attractions.name: Well 80 Brewhouse Well 80 Brewhouse
attractions.category: Brewery, Brewpub, and All-Ages Restaurant Experience Olympia
attractions.description: Well 80 is a brewery and brewpub built on the site of one of Olympia's famous historic artesian wells. The establishment uses pure 3,000-year-old artesian water exclusively in its brewing process to create a variety of craft beers. As an all-ages restaurant, it serves a menu of pizza, burgers, and sandwiches with a unique twist in a welcoming downtown setting Well 80 Brewhouse.
attractions.address: 514 4th Avenue E, Olympia, WA 98501 Well 80 Brewhouse
attractions.hours: Sunday – Thursday: 11:30 am – 10:00 pm; Friday – Saturday: 11:30 am – 11:00 pm Well 80 Brewhouse
highlights: 'Built directly on the site of historic Artesian Well #80, utilizing a subterranean reservoir for its water [Well 80 Brewhouse', 'Uses pure artesian water, carbon-dated to over 3,000 years old, for all house-brewed beers Well 80 Brewhouse', 'Offers a diverse lineup of craft beers, including recreations of historic recipes like Leopold’s #1 Lager from 1896 Washington Beer Blog', 'All-ages dining area and a dog-friendly front rail with water bowls for pets Well 80 Brewhouse FAQs', 'Features a private Barrel Room (BR) that can accommodate up to 24 people for special events Well 80 Brewhouse Reservations', 'Hosts weekly community events including Trivia Tuesdays and Small Business BINGO Wednesdays Well 80 Brewhouse FAQs']
attractions.tips: Take advantage of the daily Happy Hour from 3 PM to 5 PM for discounts on food and drinks. Be sure to check the daily specials, such as 'Public Sector Monday' for 20% off with a public ID or 'Tall Boy Tuesday' for $2 beer deals. Reservations are recommended for parties of 8 or more and are accepted Sunday through Thursday Well 80 Brewhouse FAQs.
attractions.nearby: Washington State Capitol Building and Campus, Percival Landing waterfront boardwalk, Hands On Children's Museum, Olympia Farmers Market, and Tumwater Falls Park Experience Olympia

attractions.name: Yashiro Japanese Garden City of Olympia
attractions.description: The Yashiro Japanese Garden is a serene 0.74-acre "pocket garden" in downtown Olympia, dedicated in 1990 to symbolize the sister-city relationship between Olympia and Yashiro (now Kato), Japan. Designed by landscape architect Robert Murase, the garden features traditional design principles with elements such as a koi pond, a stone pagoda, and meticulously pruned bamboo groves. Note: The garden is currently scheduled for relocation, with the current Plum Street site set to close on June 1, 2026. City of Olympia, The Olympian
attractions.address: 1010 Plum Street SE, Olympia, WA 98501 City of Olympia
attractions.hours: Monday – Sunday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Dawn to Dusk) Trip.com, City of Olympia
highlights: '18-foot tall granite pagoda [Wikipedia', 'Serene koi pond with granite lanterns The Jolt News', 'Traditional cedar entrance gates built with Japanese no-nail carpentry Wikipedia', 'Expertly pruned bamboo groves and lush moss gardens The Jolt News', 'Symbolic sister-city features representing the bond between Olympia and Kato, Japan City of Olympia']
attractions.tips: The garden is relatively small and can be experienced in a short visit; it offers a quiet escape from the surrounding city traffic. It is best visited during the spring for blossoming flowers or autumn for the colorful Japanese maples. Visitors should be aware that the current location is closing on June 1, 2026, so it is recommended to visit soon. Wanderlog, The Jolt News
attractions.nearby: Washington State Capitol, Hands On Children's Museum, Percival Landing Park, Olympia Farmers Market, and Heritage Park. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Broadway Olympia Productions
attractions.description: Broadway Olympia Productions is a professional theatrical company dedicated to bringing contemporary, high-energy Broadway-style musicals and plays to the South Puget Sound region. Shared with Theater Artists Olympia at OlyTheater, the company focuses on making arts accessible and engaging through innovative productions in a black box setting. Experience Olympia, OlyTheater
attractions.hours: Typically 8:00 PM for evening shows and 2:00 PM for matinees during production runs; Box office/venue hours vary by show. Oly Arts, OlyTheater
attractions.days_open: Performance-based (typically Friday, Saturday, Sunday during show runs). OlyTheater
attractions.admission: Standard: $22 - $25; Pay-what-you-choose options available for specific performances. Oly Arts, Experience Olympia
highlights: ['Specializes in contemporary and high-energy Broadway musicals.', 'Shares a unique collaborative performance space (OlyTheater) at Capital Mall.', 'Offers a mix of professional productions and community-focused arts engagement.', 'Features a versatile black box theater environment for intimate audience experiences.', 'Provides pay-what-you-choose performance opportunities to increase accessibility.', 'Collaborates with local organizations like the Northwest Playwrights Alliance.']
attractions.tips: Check the OlyTheater website for the most current show schedule as performances are seasonal. Arrive early to enjoy the nearby shopping and dining at Capital Mall before the show. OlyTheater
attractions.nearby: Capital Mall, Century Olympia Cinemas, Percival Landing Park, Brewery Park at Tumwater Falls. Wanderlog

attractions.name: Frye Cove County Park
attractions.description: Nestled on the west shore of Eld Inlet, Frye Cove County Park is a 67-acre destination known for its stunning views of Mount Rainier and 1,400 feet of saltwater beach access. The park offers two miles of forested trails, a playground, and picnic shelters, making it a popular spot for weddings, beachcombing, and seasonal shellfish harvesting. Thurston County, Experience Olympia
attractions.hours: 9:00 AM – Dusk daily Thurston County
attractions.admission: Free (Facility rentals for shelters available for a fee) Thurston County
highlights: ['1,400 feet of saltwater beach access on Eld Inlet', 'Scenic views of Mount Rainier on clear days', 'Over two miles of forested hiking trails', 'Seasonal shellfish harvesting for clams and oysters', 'Two reservable picnic shelters with BBQ facilities', "Children's playground and wildlife viewing opportunities"]
attractions.tips: Check the Washington Department of Fish and Wildlife (WDFW) website for current shellfish seasons and license requirements. Note that the park's steep grades and dirt paths may be challenging for those with mobility concerns. Gates are locked at dusk; ensure vehicles are removed before then. Thurston County

attractions.name: Iron Rabbit Restaurant & Bar
attractions.description: Iron Rabbit Restaurant & Bar is a family-friendly establishment located on Olympia's Westside that has been serving inspired dining since 2005. The restaurant features an open kitchen where chefs prepare creative twists on classic favorites using fresh, locally sourced ingredients in a casual and welcoming atmosphere.
attractions.hours: Sunday–Thursday: 11:30 am - 8:00 pm; Friday–Saturday: 11:30 am - 9:00 pm
highlights: ['Creative and inspired menu featuring locally sourced ingredients', 'Full-service bar with craft cocktails and rotating craft beers', 'Signature house-made root beer made right in the restaurant', "Family-friendly atmosphere with a dedicated kids' menu and rabbit-themed decor", 'Open kitchen concept allowing guests to view the culinary team in action', "Known for 'the best burgers in town' and signature Northwest Berry Cobbler"]
attractions.tips: No reservations are required for most visits, but they are accepted for parties of 6-12 and on special holidays like Valentine's Day and Mother's Day. If you're looking for a specific dessert, try their famous Northwest Berry Cobbler or the house-made root beer float. The restaurant is tucked away in a strip mall, so look closely for the sign when arriving.

attractions.name: South West Olympia Neighborhood (SWONA)
attractions.description: The South West Olympia Neighborhood is a vibrant, community-driven area known for its historic homes, walkability, and strong sense of neighborly connection. It features an eclectic mix of residential housing, local businesses, and green spaces, including the grassroots West Central Park. The neighborhood is recognized for its inclusive atmosphere and active resident participation through the South West Olympia Neighborhood Association. SWONA, ThurstonTalk

attractions.name: The Artists' Gallery
attractions.description: The Artists' Gallery is a co-operative art space owned and operated by over 25 local Washington artists. Founded in 1980, it features a diverse collection of contemporary and modern works including paintings, sculpture, jewelry, and woodcraft. The gallery rotates its exhibits every other month to provide a fresh experience for visitors and the community. (The Artists' Gallery)
attractions.address: 625 Black Lake Blvd SW (Located in Capital Mall, North Promenade)
attractions.hours: Wednesday-Saturday: 10:00am-7:00pm; Sunday: 12:00pm-5:00pm; Monday-Tuesday: Closed (The Artists' Gallery)

attractions.name: The Evergreen State College Campus Trails
attractions.description: The Evergreen State College campus features a 1,000-acre network of trails through mature second-growth forest, leading to the Puget Sound shoreline and an organic farm. These scenic paths offer a peaceful escape for hikers, birdwatchers, and students, showcasing the lush biodiversity of the Pacific Northwest. Evergreen State College, Wikipedia

It serves as a central hub for students to gain hands-on experience in sustainable agriculture, ecological stewardship, and community engagement. The farm is connected to the main Olympia campus by scenic forest trails and hosts a variety of academic programs and community events throughout the year. The Evergreen State College

attractions.name: Brewery Park at Tumwater Falls (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)
attractions.description: Brewery Park at Tumwater Falls is a 15-acre private park where the Deschutes River concludes its journey by plunging 82 feet over a series of cascading waterfalls into Capitol Lake. Managed by the non-profit Olympia Tumwater Foundation, the park offers a scenic urban oasis featuring deep basalt canyons, native gardens, and historic connections to the local brewing industry. Visitors can enjoy a peaceful environment steeped in the cultural and industrial heritage of the Pacific Northwest (Olympia Tumwater Foundation).
attractions.address: 110 Deschutes Way SW, Tumwater, WA 98501 (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:00 am – 8:00 pm (Summer Schedule); 8:00 am – 5:00 pm (Winter Schedule). Note: Entrance gate closes 30 minutes prior to park closure (Olympia Tumwater Foundation).
highlights: 'Cascading waterfalls with a total 82-foot drop ([Olympia Tumwater Foundation)', 'Half-mile scenic loop trail with views of the basalt river canyon (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)', 'Historic footbridges designed for compatibility with the surroundings (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)', 'State-run fish ladder and seasonal salmon viewing in the fall (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)', 'Iconic views of the Old Olympia Brewery and Brew Tower (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)', 'Native plant garden and historic markers throughout the grounds (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)']
attractions.tips: Dogs are welcome but must remain on a leash at all times. Be aware that while some paths are ADA accessible, parts of the canyon loop include stairs and gravel terrain. Fishing is strictly prohibited to protect the staging salmon and public safety (Olympia Tumwater Foundation).
attractions.nearby: Old Olympia Brewery, Tumwater Historical Park, Crosby House, Capitol Lake (Olympia Tumwater Foundation, City of Tumwater)

attractions.description: Brewery Park at Tumwater Falls is a 15-acre private park that features the dramatic final descent of the Deschutes River over three cascading waterfalls. Visitors can explore a scenic half-mile loop trail through a basalt rock canyon, enjoy beautifully maintained lawns and picnic areas, and view historic remnants of the local brewing industry. It serves as a premier urban oasis and tourist destination in Thurston County, maintained by a non-profit foundation. Olympia Tumwater Foundation
attractions.address: 110 Deschutes Way SW, Tumwater, WA 98501 Olympia Tumwater Foundation

highlights: 'Three cascading waterfalls plunging 82 feet into Capitol Lake [Olympia Tumwater Foundation', 'Half-mile scenic loop trail with historic footbridges and basalt rock canyon views Olympia Tumwater Foundation', 'Seasonal salmon run (late August to October) with fish ladder viewing windows Experience Olympia', 'Iconic views of the historic 1906 Olympia Brewery Washington Trails Association', "Children's playground featuring unique 1960s-era concrete ships Olympia Tumwater Foundation", "Native plant gardens and interpretive markers detailing the area's rich history Experience Olympia"]

attractions.name: Brewmaster's House (Henderson House Museum)
attractions.description: Built in 1905 for William Naumann, a German brewmaster at the Olympia Brewing Company, this Victorian-style home was remarkably modern for its time, featuring electric lights and indoor plumbing. After being acquired by the City of Tumwater in 1977, the exterior was meticulously restored to its original appearance, including its distinctive six-sided turret.
attractions.hours: Interior tours: Currently closed to the public; Exterior: Available for viewing daily City of Tumwater, Experience Olympia
attractions.admission: Free (Exterior viewing only; interior currently closed to the public) City of Tumwater, Experience Olympia
highlights: 'Built in 1905 for Olympia Brewery brewmaster William Naumann [HistoryLink', 'Features a prominent six-sided turret with red fishscale shingles National Park Service', 'Restored Queen Anne-inspired Victorian architecture City of Tumwater', 'Equipped with early modern amenities like a coal-fired boiler and electric lights Experience Olympia', 'Designated as a key landmark within the Tumwater Historic District HistoryLink']
attractions.tips: Since the interior is currently closed, visitors should focus on the architectural details from the sidewalk and read the interpretive signs that provide a history of the home and its owners. It is best visited as part of the Tumwater Historical Park loop hike. Experience Olympia, Oregon Hikers

attractions.description: Millersylvania State Park is a 903-acre recreation area featuring an old-growth forest of cedar and fir trees on the shores of Deep Lake. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the park showcases well-preserved landscape and buildings constructed by the Civilian Conservation Corps in the 1930s. It offers a serene environment for camping, hiking, and water activities just south of Washington's capital. Wikipedia, Washington State Parks

attractions.name: The Schmidt House (Olympia Tumwater Foundation)
attractions.description: The Schmidt House is an elegant 1904 Colonial Revival mansion built for Leopold Schmidt, the founder of the Olympia Brewing Company, and his wife Johanna. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the estate features picturesque grounds with native gardens, statues brought from Europe, and a reflective pool. Today, it serves as a historic site housing the Olympia Brewing Co. archives and is a popular venue for weddings and community history programs.

attractions.name: Billy Frank Jr. Nisqually National Wildlife Refuge

attractions.name: Olympic National Park
attractions.description: Olympic National Park encompasses nearly a million acres and protects a vast wilderness of several distinct ecosystems, including glacier-capped mountains, old-growth temperate rain forests, and over 70 miles of wild coastline (NPS). Designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site and International Biosphere Reserve, it serves as a critical habitat for unique species like the Roosevelt elk (Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 600 E. Park Avenue, Port Angeles, WA 98362 (NPS)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day year-round. Visitor Center hours vary by season (typically 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM or 5:00 PM) (NPS).
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $30.00, Motorcycle: $25.00, Per Person (foot/bicycle): $15.00, Olympic National Park Annual Pass: $55.00. Free entrance on select days including Memorial Day and Veterans Day (NPS).
highlights: 'Hurricane Ridge for panoramic mountain vistas and subalpine meadows ([Wildland Trekking)', 'Hoh Rain Forest featuring the iconic Hall of Mosses trail (NPS)', "Rialto Beach and its famous 'Hole in the Wall' rock formation (Miss Rover)", "The 'Tree of Life' suspended between cliffs at Kalaloch Beach (Amanda Wanders)", 'Sol Duc Falls, a dramatic multi-channel waterfall accessible via an easy forest hike (Wildland Trekking)', 'Lake Crescent, a deep, glacially carved lake known for its brilliant blue water (Miss Rover)']
attractions.tips: Plan your visit with time and distance in mind as no roads cross through the park interior (NPS). Check the road and weather hotline at (360) 565-3131 before heading out, especially for Hurricane Ridge access (NPS). Advance reservations are highly recommended for lodging and popular campgrounds during the busy summer months (NPS).
attractions.nearby: Olympic National Forest, Washington State Capitol (Olympia), Quinault Rain Forest (Wanderlog)

attractions.name: Point Defiance Park Metro Parks Tacoma
attractions.description: Point Defiance Park is a 760-acre urban jewel in Tacoma featuring natural and historical protected areas. It is home to old-growth forests, scenic waterfront views, and major attractions like the Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium and Fort Nisqually Living History Museum. Metro Parks Tacoma, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 5400 N Pearl St, Tacoma, WA 98407 Metro Parks Tacoma
attractions.hours: Open 1/2 hour before sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset daily; attraction hours vary Metro Parks Tacoma
attractions.admission: Park Entry: Free. Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium: Adult (13-64) $25 online / $30 gate, Senior (65+) $23 online / $28 gate, Youth (3-12) $17 online / $20 gate, Infant (2 & under) Free. Fort Nisqually: Adult (18-64) $13.50, Military/Senior (65+) $12.50, Youth (4-17) $10.00, Ages 3 & under Free Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium, Fort Nisqually Admission
highlights: 'Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium: The only combined zoo and aquarium in the Pacific Northwest [Point Defiance Zoo', 'Five Mile Drive & Trails: Scenic forest routes through 400 acres of old-growth forest Metro Parks Tacoma', 'Owen Beach: A popular waterfront spot with views of Mount Rainier and Vashon Island Metro Parks Tacoma', 'Fort Nisqually Living History Museum: A restored 19th-century fur trading outpost Metro Parks Tacoma', 'Japanese Garden and Pagoda: A peaceful garden featuring a historic 1914 Pagoda and Shinto shrine Wikipedia', 'Dune Peninsula: An 11-acre park on a former Superfund site offering sweeping views of Commencement Bay Metro Parks Tacoma']
attractions.tips: Parking within the park is free. The park is a cashless facility; major credit and debit cards are required for all attraction tickets. The outer loop of Five Mile Drive is permanently closed to vehicle traffic but remains accessible to pedestrians and cyclists. Buy Zoo tickets online in advance to save on admission costs. Point Defiance Zoo, Metro Parks Tacoma

attractions.name: Squaxin Island Museum, Library and Research Center
attractions.description: The Squaxin Island Museum, Library and Research Center (MLRC) tells the story of the "People of the Water" through a series of exhibits depicting the relationship between tribal members and the seven inlets of South Puget Sound. As a living eco-museum, it preserves and revitalizes the culture, identity, and traditions of the Squaxin Island Tribe for future generations (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Closed Sunday and Monday (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $5, Seniors: $4, Children (ages 5-12): $2, Children (under 5): Free (Official Website)
highlights: 'Hall of the Seven Inlets featuring seven large wall panels with tribal legends and art ([Official Website)', 'Extensive Basketry exhibit showcasing the work of master tribal weavers (Official Website)', 'Home of Sacred Belongings, a treasury of cultural items and rare artifacts (Official Website)', 'Veterans Garden, a unique tribute to tribal members who served (Tripadvisor)', 'Specialty gift shop featuring Squaxin Island and Native American art and literature (Experience Olympia)', 'Beautiful architecture designed to resemble a "Thunderbird" from local legends (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Photography is not allowed inside the museum. Visitors are encouraged to call ahead to verify hours, as the museum follows Shelton School District weather closures and may have unexpected holiday schedules (Official Website, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Little Creek Casino Resort, Salish Cliffs Golf Club, Walter Dacon Winery, Kennedy Creek Salmon Viewing Trail, and Lake Isabella State Park (Squaxin Island Tribe, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Staircase Entrance - Olympic National Park
attractions.description: Staircase is a lush, heavily forested area in the southeastern corner of Olympic National Park, featuring towering Douglas-firs and the North Fork Skokomish River. It offers a variety of hiking trails ranging from easy river loops to strenuous mountain climbs, making it a popular destination for day trips from Olympia. NPS Staircase
attractions.address: N Lake Cushman Rd, Hoodsport, WA 98548
attractions.hours: Park Entrance: Open 24 hours daily. Ranger Station: Open in summer when staffing allows (typically 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM). NPS Hours, NPS Visitor Centers
attractions.days_open: Open daily, year-round (weather permitting). NPS Hours
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $30, Motorcycle: $25, Per Person (Individual): $15, Olympic National Park Annual Pass: $55. Non-U.S. Resident Annual Pass: $250. Standard passes are valid for seven consecutive days. NPS Fees, For the Love of Wanderlust
highlights: 'Staircase Rapids Loop: An easy 2-mile trail featuring old-growth forest and a bridge over the Skokomish River. [NPS Brochure', 'The Big Cedar: A spur trail leads to a massive fallen western redcedar. NPS Brochure', 'Staircase Loop Bridge: A scenic cable bridge built in 2013 that offers stunning views of the rapids. Outdoor Society', 'High-Elevation Hikes: Access to challenging trails like Wagonwheel Lake and Flapjack Lakes. NPS Brochure', 'Skokomish River: Provides opportunities for photography, riverside picnicking, and viewing wild rapids. NPS Staircase']
attractions.tips: Check road conditions before visiting as the unpaved access road can close due to snow or weather. The park has gone cashless for entrance fees, so bring a credit or debit card. Pets and bicycles are not permitted on park trails. NPS Staircase, NPS Fees

attractions.name: Tacoma, Washington
attractions.description: Known for its world-renowned glass art and vibrant urban core, Tacoma is a cultural hub ideally situated along the saltwater banks of Puget Sound. Visitors can explore a rich museum district, stroll through 700+ acres of parkland at Point Defiance, or enjoy stunning waterfront dining along Ruston Way. Visit Pierce County, Wikipedia

attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Summer); Tuesday - Friday (Labor Day to Memorial Day) Visit Pierce County
attractions.admission: Free for city exploration and public parks like Point Defiance Park; Museum ticket prices vary (e.g., Children's Museum of Tacoma: $10–$18; Tacoma Art Museum: Free–$18) Visit Pierce County, Drugstore Divas
highlights: 'World-renowned glass art scene including the Museum of Glass and the Chihuly Bridge of Glass [Visit Pierce County', 'Point Defiance Park, a 760-acre destination with gardens, a zoo, and scenic trails Visit Pierce County', 'Museum District featuring the Washington State History Museum and Tacoma Art Museum Drugstore Divas', "LeMay

America's Car Museum, showcasing a world-class collection of classic vehicles Visit Pierce County", 'The historic Theater District for world-class performing arts Visit Pierce County', 'Ruston Way Waterfront for walking, biking, and saltwater views Visit Pierce County']
attractions.tips: Take advantage of the free Tacoma Link light rail to travel between downtown museums and restaurants. Visit the Chihuly Bridge of Glass for a free outdoor art experience, and explore 'Antique Row' for unique vintage finds. For the best views, head to Point Defiance Park at 'golden hour' for a sunset over the water. Emerald Palate, Visit Pierce County

attractions.name: Tenino Quarry Pool (Tenino Memorial Pool)
attractions.description: The Tenino Quarry Pool is a unique freshwater swimming destination set within a former sandstone quarry that was operational in the late 1800s. It features a naturally filled deep pool with depths reaching nearly 100 feet, alongside a modern chlorinated wading pool and splash pad for families. Reopened in 2024 after extensive renovations, it serves as a historic centerpiece of Tenino City Park, offering a cool 57-58 degree plunge even in mid-summer. City of Tenino, The Seattle Times
attractions.hours: June: Weekends only, Noon to 6:00 PM; July & August: Wednesday through Sunday, Noon to 6:00 PM. City of Tenino
attractions.days_open: Weekends in late June; Wednesday through Sunday in July and August. City of Tenino
attractions.admission: $4 per person for Tenino School District Residents; $6 per person for Out of District (non-residents). City of Tenino
highlights: 'Historic sandstone quarry walls and a trickling waterfall backdrop [ParentMap', 'Deep, spring-fed natural pool reaching depths of up to 100 feet The Seattle Times', 'Separate 2-foot-deep chlorinated wading pool and interactive splash pad City of Tenino', 'Diving boards for experienced swimmers in the natural pool area Experience Olympia', 'Maintains a brisk temperature of 57-58 degrees, similar to Puget Sound SFGATE', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places as part of the Tenino Stone Company Quarry Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Swimmers must be 18+ or pass a vigorous swim test (swimming 25 feet and treading water) to enter the deep quarry pool. No flotation devices or life jackets are permitted in the main quarry pool. Space for lounging is limited, so arriving early is recommended as the pool has a 200-person capacity. Water shoes are highly recommended for the wading pool ramp. City of Tenino, The Seattle Times
attractions.nearby: Tenino Depot Museum, Yelm-Rainier-Tenino Trail, Wolf Haven International, Millersylvania State Park, Sandstone Distillery. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Yelm-Rainier-Tenino Trail Thurston County
attractions.category: Rail Trail, Paved Trail Thurston County, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Yelm-Rainier-Tenino Trail is a 14.5-mile paved rail trail that runs east-west through southern Thurston County, connecting the communities of Yelm, Rainier, and Tenino. Following a former Burlington Northern Railroad line, the trail traverses diverse landscapes including forests, agricultural lands, wetlands, and creeks. It offers a flat, accessible route for pedestrians, cyclists, and equestrians, with notable views of the Deschutes River and McIntosh Lake along the way. Experience Olympia, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 105 W Yelm Ave, Yelm, WA 98597 Experience Olympia
attractions.city: Yelm (Eastern Terminus), Rainier, Tenino (Western Terminus) Thurston County
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 9:00 AM - Dusk Thurston County
highlights: '14.5-mile fully paved, flat trail ideal for all skill levels [Experience Olympia', 'Intersects with the 22-mile Chehalis Western Trail for extended regional riding Experience Olympia', 'Passes through scenic forests, wetlands, and active agricultural lands Experience Olympia', 'Features wildlife viewing opportunities, especially near McIntosh Lake Experience Olympia', 'Includes artistic landmarks like the Ponderosa wind sculpture and Centennial Bloom Wikipedia', 'Part of the Great American Rail-Trail network spanning the United States Komoot']
attractions.tips: Watch for tree root heaves, which are often marked with yellow paint for safety. Consider a stop in Rainier for local treats at the Main Street Cookie Company or lunch at Sonja's. The trail is mostly flat, making it an excellent 'first ride' of the season for all ages. TrailLink, Seattle Magazine
